codeReview.summary: The fix commit resolves both prior blocking findings from m4-mission-board-cross-surface-baseline: extension mission ordering now matches daemon canonical comparator (`updatedAt DESC`, `missionID ASC` tie-break), and extension mission lifecycle enums/contracts now represent daemon mission statuses (`draft`, `awaiting_approval`, `approved`, `dispatching`, `in_progress`, `partially_completed`, `completed`, `failed`, `cancelled`). Updated projection tests include ordering semantics and explicit equal-timestamp tie-break coverage.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The extension-worker skill's required unit-test command is broader than what this fix feature validation actually used, which can create ambiguity for focused parity fixes.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/extension-worker/SKILL.md:21-22 requires `npm --prefix extensions/openburnbar run test:unit -- test/controller.test.ts test/projections.test.ts test/workspacePanel.test.ts test/extension.test.ts`, while worker session 1032cab5-49e9-4da4-8253-d94f8cbf0e26 transcript skeleton shows scoped execution of `npm --prefix extensions/openburnbar run test:unit -- test/projections.test.ts test/extension.test.ts` plus extension-host and daemon mission tests.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared architecture lifecycle documentation is drifted from current daemon mission status contracts used by extension parity work.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md:22-26 defines lifecycle states like `planning`, `ready`, `executing`, `blocked_approval`, `reconciling`, while OpenBurnBarCore/Sources/OpenBurnBarCore/OpenBurnBarMissionControlContracts.swift:72-79 defines canonical mission statuses as `draft`, `awaiting_approval`, `approved`, `dispatching`, `in_progress`, `partially_completed`, `completed`, `failed`, `cancelled`.

summary: Pass: the fix adequately addresses the original scrutiny failure by restoring daemon-parity ordering and lifecycle status contracts in extension mission projections, with updated tests covering canonical ordering and tie-break behavior.
